target.title: fix(heartbeat): bootstrap plugin session targets
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes heartbeat target resolution for plugin channels whose runtime metadata has not been bootstrapped yet.
- Lets heartbeat `target: "last"` use the existing outbound plugin bootstrap path before deciding that a recovered session route has no target.
- Keeps plugin-owned target and account validation in the same resolver path, so invalid targets and unknown accounts are still suppressed instead of being delivered raw.

## Linked context

Closes #43216

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Heartbeat delivery to a plugin channel can recover the last session route after plugin metadata bootstrap, without bypassing plugin target/account policy.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on Node v24.14.0 in WSL2, using the real heartbeat target resolver and bundled Telegram channel metadata.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'EOF'
import { resolveHeartbeatDeliveryTarget } from './src/infra/outbound/targets.ts';

const cases = [
  {
    name: 'telegram group target from last session route',
    entry: { sessionId: 'main', updatedAt: 1, lastChannel: 'telegram', lastTo: '-1001234567890', chatType: 'group' },
    heartbeat: { target: 'last' },
  },
  {
    name: 'invalid explicit account remains suppressed',
    entry: { sessionId: 'main', updatedAt: 1, lastChannel: 'telegram', lastTo: '-1001234567890', chatType: 'group' },
    heartbeat: { target: 'last', accountId: 'missing-account' },
  },
];
for (const item of cases) {
  const result = resolveHeartbeatDeliveryTarget({ cfg: {}, entry: item.entry, heartbeat: item.heartbeat });
  console.log(JSON.stringify({ name: item.name, result }, null, 2));
}
EOF
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
{
  "name": "telegram group target from last session route",
  "result": {
    "channel": "telegram",
    "to": "-1001234567890",
    "chatType": "group",
    "lastChannel": "telegram"
  }
}
{
  "name": "invalid explicit account remains suppressed",
  "result": {
    "channel": "none",
    "reason": "unknown-account",
    "accountId": "missing-account",
    "lastChannel": "telegram"
  }
}
```

- Observed result after fix: The last Telegram session route resolves to a deliverable heartbeat target, while an invalid explicit account still returns `channel: "none"` with `reason: "unknown-account"`.
- What was not tested: No live Telegram message was sent; this PR only changes target resolution before send.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The proof exercises the source resolver path directly rather than a credentialed channel delivery.
- Before evidence: The new regression test failed before the implementation with `expected 'forum' to be 'none'` / `expected 'none' to be 'forum'` on the missing bootstrap/policy cases, confirming the resolver either dropped the recovered route or bypassed validation.

## Tests and validation

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/targets.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/infra/outbound/targets.ts src/infra/outbound/targets.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base upstream/main --parallel-tests "OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/targets.test.ts src/infra/heartbeat-runner.returns-default-unset.test.ts"`

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? `Yes`

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? `No`

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? `No`

Highest-risk area: Heartbeat delivery target selection for plugin channels during startup or registry bootstrap.

How is that risk mitigated? The change opts into the existing outbound bootstrap path and keeps target/account validation in plugin-owned resolvers before returning a deliverable target.

## Current review state

Next action: maintainer review and CI.

AI-assisted (Codex). Proof above was run manually.
